title: Product Manager, YouTube Create
role_summary: PM for YouTube Create — a consumer-facing creator tool product within YouTube, focused on enabling content creation at scale.
why_good_fit: Jay's creator economy experience is directly transferable: he scaled Resso's creator-facing features, led TikTok SoundOn music distribution, built the Ghost Music Tab for artist activation, and worked on Meta's creator monetisation and Shoppertainment strategy. His deep understanding of Indian regional/long-tail creators and GenZ content consumption (GenZ Product Council member) makes him uniquely suited. 5 years PM experience required — Jay has 7 years, perfect seniority match.
how_to_win: Lead with the Resso founding story (alpha → India's largest music streaming app) and the creator-centric product thesis. Highlight the Ghost Music Tab as a zero-to-one creator tool. Connect Meta's Shoppertainment and creator growth work to YouTube Create's mission. Emphasise field-level understanding of how Indian creators actually produce content — voice-led, mobile-first, regional language. Show AI fluency as a lever for creator tool innovation.
job_description: You'll own the product roadmap for YouTube Create, a suite of tools that help creators produce, edit, and publish content on YouTube. Day-to-day involves defining features based on creator needs, working with engineering and design to ship, and measuring impact on creator output and engagement. Success means more creators producing higher-quality content more frequently.

title: Product Manager, YouTube Live Living Room
role_summary: PM for YouTube's live streaming experience on TV/living room devices — a consumer video product at the intersection of live content and large-screen viewing.
why_good_fit: Jay has direct live streaming and video product experience from Resso and TikTok. His understanding of Indian consumer video consumption patterns (regional content, live engagement) is highly relevant. Only 3 years PM experience required — Jay is overqualified in the best way, bringing senior-level insight. His Shoppertainment work at Meta (convergence of entertainment and commerce) maps to live commerce/engagement on living room screens. GenZ Product Council membership shows deep understanding of evolving content consumption.
how_to_win: Frame the Resso story as understanding how users consume media in lean-back vs. lean-forward modes. Highlight the Shoppertainment thesis — live content as a commerce and engagement driver. Discuss Indian living room dynamics: shared screens, regional language content, cricket/live events. Show how field research methodology (20+ AI user interviews, bakery SMB discovery) translates to understanding living room user behaviour. Position as someone who can bring India market intelligence to a global product surface.
job_description: You'll own the YouTube Live experience on TV and living room devices — defining how live streams are discovered, consumed, and interacted with on large screens. Day-to-day involves working with engineering, UX, and content teams to ship features that increase live viewership and engagement on connected TVs. Success is measured by live watch time and user engagement on living room surfaces.

title: Product Manager, Google Pay
role_summary: PM for Google Pay — India's leading consumer payments product, deeply tied to India market growth, SMBs, and financial inclusion.
why_good_fit: Google Pay is fundamentally an India market growth product — Jay's core superpower. His SMB work at Meta (bakery insight → 2.3% India revenue uplift, 10M businesses impacted) directly maps to Google Pay's merchant/SMB ecosystem. He understands how Indian informal businesses actually transact — voice-led, WhatsApp-based, low digital literacy. His Shoppertainment and commerce convergence work is relevant to Google Pay's commerce ambitions. 5 years PM experience required — Jay has 7, ideal seniority.
how_to_win: Lead with the Dahod bakery story — it's a perfect Google Pay narrative about understanding how real Indian SMBs handle payments and digital tools. Highlight the 2.3% revenue uplift and the agentic resolution system as evidence of building products that serve overlooked Indian users. Connect Resso's Tier 2/3 thesis to Google Pay's expansion into deeper India. Show understanding of UPI ecosystem, informal commerce patterns, and voice-led business operations.
job_description: You'll own product features within Google Pay, India's most-used payments app. Day-to-day involves identifying user needs across consumers and merchants, defining product specs, working with engineering to ship, and measuring adoption and transaction growth. Success means driving payments volume, merchant activation, and user engagement across India's diverse economic landscape.

title: Product Manager, eWallets
role_summary: PM for Google's eWallet products — consumer fintech product focused on digital wallet experiences, likely with strong India/emerging market relevance.
why_good_fit: eWallets in India serve the exact user segments Jay knows deeply — SMBs, Tier 2/3 consumers, informal businesses. His Meta SMB work (10M businesses, agentic resolution, 2.3% revenue uplift) demonstrates ability to build for financially underserved Indian users. Resso's Tier 2/3 growth thesis shows he can drive adoption in non-metro India. 5 years PM experience required — Jay has 7. His field research methodology is perfect for understanding wallet usage patterns.
how_to_win: Position the bakery/SMB story as understanding the real payments and wallet behaviour of Indian small businesses. Highlight how Indian SMBs use WhatsApp as their de facto business tool — and how wallet products need to integrate into that workflow. Show Resso's success in Tier 2/3 India as evidence of building products for non-metro users. Emphasise zero-to-one experience (Resso founding team, travel startup) as relevant for building new wallet features.
job_description: You'll define and ship eWallet product features within Google's payments ecosystem. Day-to-day involves understanding user wallet behaviour, defining product requirements, working with engineering and design, and measuring adoption metrics. Success means growing wallet usage, transaction frequency, and user retention across target markets.

title: Product Manager, Payments Risk
role_summary: PM for payments risk — managing fraud detection, enforcement, and risk systems within Google's payments products.
why_good_fit: Jay's single most impactful project at Meta was literally about payments/enforcement risk — the SMB over-enforcement discovery that led to 2.3% India revenue uplift. He identified false positives in enforcement models, mapped the user journey of blocked SMBs, influenced policy changes, and got a new product team created. This is almost a 1:1 match. His understanding of how global enforcement models fail in India-specific contexts is exactly what a Payments Risk PM needs. 3 years PM experience required — Jay has 7, strong overqualification.
how_to_win: This is Jay's strongest story-to-role match. Lead with the full SMB over-enforcement narrative: casual bakery conversation → data analysis revealing false positives → policy change → agentic resolution system → 10M businesses impacted → 2.3% revenue uplift. Frame it as: 'I've already done this exact job — identified where risk models over-enforce on legitimate Indian users, and built the product solution.' Show understanding of India-specific risk patterns (informal businesses, different CRM usage, voice-led operations).
job_description: You'll own risk management products within Google's payments ecosystem — building systems that detect fraud while minimising false positives that block legitimate users. Day-to-day involves analysing risk patterns, defining enforcement thresholds, working with engineering on detection models, and balancing security with user experience. Success means reducing fraud losses while maintaining low false-positive rates.

title: Product Manager, Payments Platform
role_summary: PM for Google's payments platform infrastructure — the underlying systems powering Google Pay and other payment products.
why_good_fit: Jay's SMB enforcement work at Meta required deep understanding of platform-level payment and business account systems. His 2.3% India revenue uplift came from understanding how platform-level enforcement decisions impact real businesses. His experience with WhatsApp API, Paid Messaging, and advertiser conversion shows platform-level product thinking. 5 years PM experience required — Jay has 7. India-based payments platform role aligns with his India market expertise.
how_to_win: Lead with the SMB enforcement story as a platform-level product insight — how platform decisions cascade to millions of users. Highlight experience working across Meta's business products (WhatsApp API, Paid Messaging, advertising tools) as evidence of platform thinking. Show understanding of India's payments infrastructure (UPI, merchant ecosystem). Emphasise the methodology: field observation → data analysis → platform-level product recommendation → execution.
job_description: You'll own components of Google's payments platform — the infrastructure that enables payment transactions across Google products. Day-to-day involves defining platform capabilities, working with engineering on reliability and scalability, and ensuring the platform serves diverse payment use cases. Success means platform uptime, transaction success rates, and enabling new payment features.

title: Product Manager, Google Play Apps

title: Product Manager, AI Gaming, Google Play
role_summary: PM for AI-powered gaming experiences within Google Play — at the intersection of AI product development and consumer gaming.

His zero-to-one experience (Resso, travel startup) is relevant for building new AI gaming features. 5 years PM experience required — Jay has 7. Success means AI features that meaningfully improve the gaming experience for users and developers.

title: Product Manager, Applied AI, Google Cloud
role_summary: PM for Applied AI products within Google Cloud — bringing GenAI/LLM capabilities to enterprise customers through cloud products.

His MetaAI India field work (20+ user interviews, language-as-growth-lever insight) shows ability to translate AI capabilities into product decisions. Requires GenAI/LLM domain experience — Jay has this from both Meta and personal AI tool usage. 5 years PM experience required — Jay has 7. Gap: this is B2B/Cloud, not consumer, and Jay's enterprise experience is limited.
how_to_win: Lead with the personal AI operating system story — building multiple agents, automating 80% of tasks, top 5% usage. Highlight the MetaAI India insight about language as a product growth lever for AI — this shows product intuition about how AI products succeed. job_description: You'll own Applied AI product features within Google Cloud — defining how enterprise customers use GenAI and LLM capabilities. Success means enterprise customers successfully deploying AI solutions through Google Cloud.
